summary: Drew DeVault's June 2019 status update covers progress across his many free software projects. He introduces openring, a tool for bloggers to cross-promote via RSS without centralized platforms, and mentions his $20 offer to encourage new bloggers. He celebrates the aerc email client's initial release and its growing community, updates on sway/wlroots including VR support plans, and reports SourceHut milestones like todo.sr.ht gaining an API. The post reflects his productivity and satisfaction working on FOSS full-time.
key_points: Openring promotes a decentralized network of bloggers supporting each other through RSS, avoiding nonfree or centralized software
The aerc email client shipped its first pre-release and quickly attracted community patches, with 0.2.0 already being planned
wlroots is gaining VR support via HTC Vive hardware, with ambitions toward a full free software VR stack using OpenXR and OpenHMD
SourceHut's todo.sr.ht gained an API and webhooks, unblocking the dispatch.sr.ht overhaul for features like building mailing list patches and linking tickets to commits
The RISC-V musl libc port is heading upstream, marking a concrete contribution to the RISC-V ecosystem
money_quotes: I hope to use this to encourage the growth of a network of bloggers supporting each other without any nonfree or centralized software.
I've also made an open offer to give $20 to anyone who wants to make their own blog, and so far 5 new blogs have taken me up on the offer.
I'm tremendously excited about this project, I think it has heaps of potential and a community is quickly forming to help us live up to it.
I've never been busier, happier, and more productive than I have been since working on FOSS full-time.
This'll open the door for features like building patches sent to mailing lists, linking tickets to commits, and much more.
I also would like to contribute with the OpenXR and OpenHMD efforts for bringing a full-stack free software solution for VR.
key_insight: DeVault's prolific output across decentralized blogging tools, email clients, Wayland compositors, and SourceHut illustrates his vision of building an entire free software ecosystem as alternatives to centralized platforms.
